chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Constitutional Petition No.D- 2833 of 2022 Date Order with Signature of Judge 1. For order on CMA No.13176/2022 (Urgency) 2. For order as to maintainability 17.5.2022 Mr. Manzoor Hameed Arain, advocate for the petitioners Mr. Ali Safdar Depar, AAG -------------------------------- 1. Learned counsel for the petitioner pleads urgency in the matter. Urgent application is granted. Counsel for the petitioners referred to the order dated 11.5.2022 passed by this Court in C.P. No. D-2805/2022 and submitted that the impugned order to the extent of the petitioners may be kept in abeyance. Let comments be filed by the respondents. Meanwhile, if the petitioners have not been relieved from the present posting, the impugned order to the extent of the petitioners shall be kept in abeyance. To come up on 23.5.2022 at 11:00 a.m., when I.G. Police Sindh or any Police Officer well conversant with the facts of the case, shall be in attendance to assist this Court. AAG waives notice and submits that the decision taken by the I.G. Police Sindh is in compliance with the orders passed by the Hon’ble Supreme Court as such the impugned order needs not to be interfered by this Court on the premise that if the petitioners in all connected petitions of similar nature are aggrieved, they may approach the Hon’ble Supreme Court in review petition and not this Court. Prima facie, the plea taken by learned AAG is tenable, therefore, all the learned counsel, representing the petitioners in similar petitions as well as AAG are directed to come prepared to assist this Court on the subject questions on the next date of hearing and in case they failed to turn up and argue the matter, the interim arrangement in all petitions shall stand recalled without further notice to all concerned.

Police Sindh is in compliance with the orders passed by the Hon’ble Supreme Court as such the impugned order needs not to be interfered by this Court on the premise that if the petitioners in all connected petitions of similar nature are aggrieved, they may approach the Hon’ble Supreme Court in review petition and not this Court. Prima facie, the plea taken by learned AAG is tenable, therefore, all the learned counsel, representing the petitioners in similar petitions as well as AAG are directed to come prepared to assist this Court on the subject questions on the next date of hearing and in case they failed to turn up and argue the matter, the interim arrangement in all petitions shall stand recalled without further notice to all concerned. Office is directed to fix CP No. D-2805/2022 and all the matters of similar nature on the aforesaid date with intimation notice to all concerned. However, it is made clear that the questions involved in all petitions are legal and the matter could be heard and shall be decided on the next date of hearing, based on the material available on record. Office is directed to communicate this order to the I.G. Police Sindh as well as to the counsel for the petitioners in all identical petitions. J U D G E J U D G E Zahid/*
